description_long: This is a medium-sized wooden sailing ship with a streamlined hull and a pointed bow, designed for efficient movement through water. The hull is constructed from planks with a natural wood finish, and the deck is open and flat, lacking any visible cabins or superstructure. The ship features two tall, vertical masts, each with several horizontal spars for supporting sails, though no sails are currently present. The stern is slightly squared off, providing a stable rear section, while the bow is sharply angled for cutting through waves. The deck is largely unobstructed, with only the masts and their supports rising above the surface. There are no visible lifeboats, anchors, or other deck equipment, and the ship lacks any registration markings or decorative elements.
description: A wooden sailing ship with two tall masts, a pointed bow, and an open, flat deck. The hull is streamlined and natural wood in color, with no visible superstructure or equipment.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pointed bow, central alignment of the masts, and the narrow, streamlined hull.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the hull, the two upright masts, and the flat, open deck.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the squared stern, the alignment of the masts, and the overall symmetry of the ship.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the hull's profile and the placement of the masts.
attribute_hull_design: The hull is a single displacement type with a pointed bow and a squared stern, constructed from wooden planks. It has a deep keel running along the centerline, providing stability and directional control. The hull is smooth and streamlined, with no visible bulbous bow or additional keels.
attribute_superstructure_layout: There is no visible superstructure; the deck is open and flat with no cabins, bridges, or towers. The only vertical elements are the two masts rising from the deck.
